REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE HONORABLE VICTOR M. HWANG RECUSES HIMSELF IN THIS MATTER. THE MATTER IS HEARD BY THE HONORABLE GARRETT L. WONG. THE MATTER IS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON AUGUST 5, 2025, IN GOOD FAITH TO ADDRESS THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER. ADDITIONALLY, THE DEMURRER WAS FILED MAY 23, 2025 AND SET FOR HEARING ON JULY 23, 2025. "DEMURRERS MUST BE SET FOR HEARING NOT MORE THAN 35 DAYS FOLLOWING THE FILING OF THE DEMURRER OR ON THE FIRST DATE AVAILABLE TO THE COURT THEREAFTER. FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, THE COURT MAY ORDER THE HEARING HELD ON AN EARLIER OR LATER DAY ON NOTICE PRESCRIBED BY THE COURT." (CRC 3.1320(D)). DEFENDANT DID NOT PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION FOR THE SELECTED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO EXPUNGE NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION

Appearances: Kathryn Moorer of Wright Finlay & Zak (949.477.5050) for defendants and moving parties; Kurt Grimes (415.312.8278) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona.

The court strikes plaintiff's supplemental filings that were filed this morning. Ms. Moorer indicates she did not receive notice of plaintiff's intent to argue.

The matter is argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Plaintiff has not shown a probably valid claim affecting title to the property. The initial Complaint and FAC were filed in April 2025 and alleged that a foreclosure "is scheduled for February 27, 2025." (Complaint, FAC 4). Additionally, causes of action 1, 2, and 6 (wrongful foreclosure, fraud, and quiet title) fail to show a probably valid claim affecting title because Plaintiff is impermissibly shifting burdens onto Defendants, Plaintiff's claims regarding securitization fail as a matter of law, and Plaintiff's evidence is solely limited to contesting the validity of deeds to which Defendants were not parties. Causes of action 3-5 (California Homeowner Bill of Rights, Truth in Lending Act, and Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act) do not affect title because Plaintiff's only remedy is damages. Cause of action 7 (attorney malfeasance and breach of fiduciary duty) does not affect title, has no evidentiary support in this motion, and is contradicted by Exhibit B of the FAC where Plaintiff authorizes dismissal of the prior lawsuit. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Appearances: Katherine Moorer of Wright Finlay & Zak, LLP (949.477.5050) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

The Honorable Victor M. Hwang recuses himself in this matter. The matter is heard by the Honorable Garrett L. Wong. The matter is not argued. The court modifies its ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff is ordered to meet and confer by close of business on August 5, 2025, in good faith to address the issues raised by the demurrer. Additionally, the demurrer was filed May 23, 2025 and set for hearing on July 23, 2025.
